question: I want to build a streaming/recurring-payments contract on Soroban — what existing Stellar projects, repos, or SCF submissions should I study?

golden.answer:
Study prior art as a **dated maturity matrix**, not a bare repo list:

- **SStream** — Rust create/claim/cancel stream code and SCF #16 $36K Awarded; linked UI was 404 and no public contract was verified.
- **Tide Stream Payment / Zentra** — Rust contract/tests/frontend and SCF #23 $18.2K Awarded; frontend responded, but no mainnet contract was verified.
- **Fluxity** — Soroban streaming contract/tests and an official SCF #21 $68K Awarded row; do not turn its internally inconsistent project aggregate into a second award; deployment remained inconclusive.
- **Paystreme** — SCF #11 $50K Awarded proposal prior art; current public contract/source trail was not verified.
- **ApiCharge** — substantial subscription/payment code, SCF #40 $79K Awarded, and a real pubnet contract; its custom time-subscription/x402-compatible scheme and runtime maturity must not be mislabeled canonical x402 exact.
- **RouteDock/MPP session examples** — substantial testnet/reference channel and agent-vault work, not verified mainnet production.

For every result, show repo, official submission, UI status, contract/network, audit/source-verification status, and as-of date. Discovery does not prove production readiness.
keyFacts: Returns real streaming/recurring-payment prior art with code and official provenance.
Separates funding, UI, contract/network, and production maturity for each item.
avoid: Do NOT invent repos or treat repository/SCF presence as current production deployment.
Do NOT collapse x402, MPP sessions/channels, and custom recurring-payment contracts into one standard.
golden.notes:
GROUNDED + CORRECTED: live Scout repo search (2026-06-22, /api/repos/search) does NOT surface the Phase-1 illustrative names (FlowFi, Quipay, stellar-drips). Real streaming/recurring-payment prior art: luanlabs/fluxity-interface (token streaming), tosinshada/tide-streaming, Handilusa/Ferrule (MPP session channels), and x402/MPP payment-execution repos (winsznx/routedock, davidmaronio/StellarPay402). Do NOT hard-gate on a single exact name set — the gate is 'real Scout-grounded repos with provenance', and must_avoid forbids invented repos. 2026-06-29 RE-VERIFIED on live Scout + GitHub: CORRECTED handle luanlabs/fluxity -> luanlabs/fluxity-interface (bare 'luanlabs/fluxity' 404s; the luanlabs/fluxity-* family is fluxity-interface (Scout 28, desc 'token streaming solution'), fluxity-v1-core (Soroban contract V1), fluxity-api, fluxity.finance). Confirmed Scout-graded: tosinshada/tide-streaming (in corpus), Handilusa/Ferrule (repoScore 53, desc 'Pay-per-token AI agents...MPP session channels'), winsznx/routedock (repoScore 69, 'unified payment execution layer for x402/MPP'), davidmaronio/StellarPay402 (repoScore 85). Source github.com/luanlabs/fluxity-interface resolves 200.
Also good if the answer: References relevant SCF submissions or an SDF/MPP recurring-payments writeup as study material.
Golden cites: Scout repo / SCF submission provenance for each surfaced item.
GT-18 CORRECTION 2026-07-10: grade a code/SCF/UI/network/source-verification matrix. The inherited repo-only roster and deployment implications are superseded.

corroboration.claim: SStream, Tide/Zentra, Fluxity, Paystreme, and ApiCharge are real Stellar streaming/recurring-payment prior art with distinct code/funding/deployment states.

verified.by: Golden-truth sweep import — The inherited prior-art list mixed interface repos, MPP/x402 references, and streaming contracts without separating code, official funding, UI reachability, network deployment, or source verification. Independent review found omitted projects and materially stronger testnet/pubnet evidence for several entries.

rootCause: Repository discovery was treated as deployment proof and a static handle list replaced a maturity comparison.
